summary: nock-helper postinstall exfiltrates npm tokens, env secrets, git credentials, and crypto wallets to hardcoded C2
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js runs automatically and silently harvests installer-side secrets: it reads ~/.npmrc (including npm auth tokens), ~/.env (parsing API keys, DB URLs, cloud/webhook/payment secrets), and ~/.git-credentials; enumerates 71 Chromium/Brave/Edge/Firefox crypto-wallet browser extension LevelDB directories on Linux/macOS/Windows to extract vault/mnemonic/privateKey/seed/password material; and scans ~/Documents, ~/Desktop, ~/Downloads for files whose names or contents match crypto-wallet/seed-phrase patterns. The harvested data (hostname, username, timestamp, credentials, wallet contents, seed-phrase file previews) is POSTed as JSON over plaintext HTTP to a hardcoded bare-IP C2 at http://149.28.127.35:8888. Header comments in the file explicitly describe it as a 'Token harvester + Crypto wallet scanner' that runs 'Silent. Zero trace.' The package's advertised purpose (a lodash-style utility helper, named to leverage the popular `nock` HTTP mocking library) is entirely cover; index.js is a dummy module with a comment stating the real payload is in postinstall.js.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
